root.description: A map of additional tags to add to the IAM role created
root.name: iam_role_tags

root.description: Launch template version. Can be version number, `$Latest`, or `$Default`
root.name: launch_template_version

root.description: The minimum size of the autoscaling group
root.name: min_size

root.description: Determines whether to use `name` as is or create a unique name beginning with the `name` as the prefix
root.name: use_name_prefix

root.description: Allows setting instance protection. The autoscaling group will not select instances with this setting for termination during scale in events.
root.name: protect_from_scale_in

root.description: The attribute requirements for the type of instance. If present then `instance_type` cannot be present
root.name: instance_requirements

root.description: Determines whether to use `launch_template_name` as is or create a unique name beginning with the `launch_template_name` as the prefix
root.name: launch_template_use_name_prefix

root.description: The network performance options for the launch template
root.name: network_performance_options

root.description: The Base64-encoded user data to provide when launching the instance
root.name: user_data

root.description: A list of metrics to collect. The allowed values are `GroupDesiredCapacity`, `GroupInServiceCapacity`, `GroupPendingCapacity`, `GroupMinSize`, `GroupMaxSize`, `GroupInServiceInstances`, `GroupPendingInstances`, `GroupStandbyInstances`, `GroupStandbyCapacity`, `GroupTerminatingCapacity`, `GroupTerminatingInstances`, `GroupTotalCapacity`, `GroupTotalInstances`
root.name: enabled_metrics

root.description: Determines whether the `desired_capacity` value is ignored after initial apply. See README note for more details
root.name: ignore_desired_capacity_changes

root.description: A list of processes to suspend for the Auto Scaling Group. The allowed values are `Launch`, `Terminate`, `HealthCheck`, `ReplaceUnhealthy`, `AZRebalance`, `AlarmNotification`, `ScheduledActions`, `AddToLoadBalancer`, `InstanceRefresh`. Note that if you suspend either the `Launch` or `Terminate` process types, it can prevent your Auto Scaling Group from functioning properly
root.name: suspended_processes

root.description: The CPU options for the instance
root.name: cpu_options

root.description: If true, enables EC2 instance termination protection
root.name: disable_api_termination

root.description: The market (purchasing) option for the instance
root.name: instance_market_options

root.description: Secondary interfaces to associate with instances launched from the template
root.name: secondary_interfaces
root.type:
list(object({
    delete_on_termination    = optional(bool)
    device_index             = optional(number)
    interface_type           = optional(string)
    network_card_index       = optional(number)
    private_ip_address_count = optional(number)
    private_ip_addresses     = optional(list(string))
    secondary_subnet_id      = optional(string)
  }))

root.description: The name of the IAM instance profile to be created (`create_iam_instance_profile` = `true`) or existing (`create_iam_instance_profile` = `false`)
root.name: iam_instance_profile_name

root.description: Time (in seconds) after instance comes into service before checking health
root.name: health_check_grace_period

root.description: The ARN of the service-linked role that the ASG will use to call other AWS services
root.name: service_linked_role_arn

root.description: Name that is propogated to launched EC2 instances via a tag - if not provided, defaults to `var.name`
root.name: instance_name

root.description: A maximum duration that Terraform should wait for ASG instances to be healthy before timing out. (See also Waiting for Capacity below.) Setting this to '0' causes Terraform to skip all Capacity Waiting behavior.
root.name: wait_for_capacity_timeout

root.description: The kernel ID
root.name: kernel_id

root.description: The maintenance options for the instance
root.name: maintenance_options

root.description: A map of additional tags to add to the launch template
root.name: launch_template_tags

root.description: A list of policies to decide how the instances in the Auto Scaling Group should be terminated. The allowed values are `OldestInstance`, `NewestInstance`, `OldestLaunchConfiguration`, `ClosestToNextInstanceHour`, `OldestLaunchTemplate`, `AllocationStrategy`, `Default`
root.name: termination_policies

root.description: The type of the instance. If present then `instance_requirements` cannot be present
root.name: instance_type

root.description: The placement of the instance
root.name: placement
root.type:
object({
    affinity                = optional(string)
    availability_zone       = optional(string)
    group_id                = optional(string)
    group_name              = optional(string)
    host_id                 = optional(string)
    host_resource_group_arn = optional(string)
    partition_number        = optional(number)
    spread_domain           = optional(string)
    tenancy                 = optional(string)
  })

root.description: Determines whether an IAM instance profile is created or to use an existing IAM instance profile
root.name: create_iam_instance_profile

root.description: Name to use on IAM role created
root.name: iam_role_name

root.description: IAM role path
root.name: iam_role_path

root.description: ARN of the policy that is used to set the permissions boundary for the IAM role
root.name: iam_role_permissions_boundary

root.description: The unit of measurement for the value specified for desired_capacity. Supported for attribute-based instance type selection only. Valid values: `units`, `vcpu`, `memory-mib`.
root.name: desired_capacity_type

root.description: If this block is configured, add a Warm Pool to the specified Auto Scaling group
root.name: warm_pool

root.description: Indicates whether capacity rebalance is enabled
root.name: capacity_rebalance

root.description: ID of an existing launch template to be used (created outside of this module)
root.name: launch_template_id

root.description: Setting this causes Terraform to wait for this number of instances to show up healthy in the ELB only on creation. Updates will not wait on ELB instance number changes
root.name: min_elb_capacity

root.description: Timeout configurations for the autoscaling group
root.name: timeouts

root.description: Description of the launch template
root.name: launch_template_description

root.description: If true, enables EC2 instance stop protection
root.name: disable_api_stop

root.description: A map of configuration for capacity distribution across availability zones
root.name: availability_zone_distribution

root.description: Whether to ignore failed Auto Scaling scaling activities while waiting for capacity. The default is false -- failed scaling activities cause errors to be returned.
root.name: ignore_failed_scaling_activities

root.description: Setting this will cause Terraform to wait for exactly this number of healthy instances in all attached load balancers on both create and update operations. Takes precedence over `min_elb_capacity` behavior.
root.name: wait_for_elb_capacity

root.description: Specify volumes to attach to the instance besides the volumes specified by the AMI
root.name: block_device_mappings
root.type:
list(object({
    device_name = optional(string)
    ebs = optional(object({
      delete_on_termination      = optional(bool)
      encrypted                  = optional(bool)
      iops                       = optional(number)
      kms_key_id                 = optional(string)
      snapshot_id                = optional(string)
      throughput                 = optional(number)
      volume_initialization_rate = optional(number)
      volume_size                = optional(number)
      volume_type                = optional(string)
    }))
    no_device    = optional(string)
    virtual_name = optional(string)
  }))

root.description: The ID of the ram disk
root.name: ram_disk_id

root.description: Map of target scaling policy schedule to create
root.name: scaling_policies

root.description: Allows deleting the Auto Scaling Group without waiting for all instances in the pool to terminate. You can force an Auto Scaling Group to delete even if it's in the process of scaling a resource. Normally, Terraform drains all the instances before deleting the group. This bypasses that behavior and potentially leaves resources dangling
root.name: force_delete

root.description: Determines whether to create autoscaling group or not
root.name: create

root.description: A map of tags to assign to resources
root.name: tags

root.description: `EC2` or `ELB`. Controls how health checking is done
root.name: health_check_type

root.description: One or more Lifecycle Hooks to attach to the Auto Scaling Group before instances are launched. The syntax is exactly the same as the separate `aws_autoscaling_lifecycle_hook` resource, without the `autoscaling_group_name` attribute. Please note that this will only work when creating a new Auto Scaling Group. For all other use-cases, please use `aws_autoscaling_lifecycle_hook` resource
root.name: initial_lifecycle_hooks
root.type:
list(object({
    default_result          = optional(string)
    heartbeat_timeout       = optional(number)
    lifecycle_transition    = string
    name                    = string
    notification_metadata   = optional(string)
    notification_target_arn = optional(string)
    role_arn                = optional(string)
  }))

root.description: The maximum amount of time, in seconds, that an instance can be in service, values must be either equal to 0 or between 86400 and 31536000 seconds
root.name: max_instance_lifetime

root.description: The name of the placement group into which you'll launch your instances, if any
root.name: placement_group

root.description: Customize the metadata options for the instance
root.name: metadata_options
root.type:
object({
    http_endpoint               = optional(string, "enabled")
    http_protocol_ipv6          = optional(string)
    http_put_response_hop_limit = optional(number, 1)
    http_tokens                 = optional(string, "required")
    instance_metadata_tags      = optional(string)
  })

root.description: If this block is configured, start an Instance Refresh when this Auto Scaling Group is updated
root.name: instance_refresh

root.description: A list of tag keys that should NOT be propagated to launched EC2 instances
root.name: autoscaling_group_tags_not_propagate_at_launch

root.description: Default Version of the launch template
root.name: default_version

root.description: Customize network interfaces to be attached at instance boot time
root.name: network_interfaces
root.type:
list(object({
    associate_carrier_ip_address = optional(bool)
    associate_public_ip_address  = optional(bool)
    connection_tracking_specification = optional(object({
      tcp_established_timeout = optional(number)
      udp_stream_timeout      = optional(number)
      udp_timeout             = optional(number)
    }))
    delete_on_termination = optional(bool)
    description           = optional(string)
    device_index          = optional(number)
    ena_srd_specification = optional(object({
      ena_srd_enabled = optional(bool)
      ena_srd_udp_specification = optional(object({
        ena_srd_udp_enabled = optional(bool)
      }))
    }))
    interface_type       = optional(string)
    ipv4_address_count   = optional(number)
    ipv4_addresses       = optional(list(string))
    ipv4_prefix_count    = optional(number)
    ipv4_prefixes        = optional(list(string))
    ipv6_address_count   = optional(number)
    ipv6_addresses       = optional(list(string))
    ipv6_prefix_count    = optional(number)
    ipv6_prefixes        = optional(list(string))
    network_card_index   = optional(number)
    network_interface_id = optional(string)
    primary_ipv6         = optional(bool)
    private_ip_address   = optional(string)
    security_groups      = optional(list(string), [])
    subnet_id            = optional(string)
  }))

root.description: Whether to update Default Version each update. Conflicts with `default_version`
root.name: update_default_version

root.description: Determines whether the IAM role name (`iam_role_name`) is used as a prefix
root.name: iam_role_use_name_prefix

root.description: The amount of time, in seconds, after a scaling activity completes before another scaling activity can start
root.name: default_cooldown

root.description: The maximum size of the autoscaling group
root.name: max_size

root.description: The granularity to associate with the metrics to collect. The only valid value is `1Minute`
root.name: metrics_granularity

root.description: A map of additional tags to add to the autoscaling group
root.name: autoscaling_group_tags

root.description: Determines whether to create launch template or not
root.name: create_launch_template

root.description: Shutdown behavior for the instance. Can be `stop` or `terminate`. (Default: `stop`)
root.name: instance_initiated_shutdown_behavior

root.description: A list of security group IDs to associate
root.name: security_groups

root.description: Do you agree that Putin doesn't respect Ukrainian sovereignty and territorial integrity? More info: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Putin_khuylo!
root.name: putin_khuylo

root.description: A list of Availability Zones where instances in the Auto Scaling group can be created. Used for launching into the default VPC subnet in each Availability Zone when not using the `vpc_zone_identifier` attribute, or for attaching a network interface when an existing network interface ID is specified in a launch template. Conflicts with `vpc_zone_identifier`
root.name: availability_zones

root.description: Customize the credit specification of the instance
root.name: credit_specification

root.description: If true, the launched EC2 instance will be EBS-optimized
root.name: ebs_optimized

root.description: The hibernation options for the instance
root.name: hibernation_options

root.description: The key name that should be used for the instance
root.name: key_name

root.description: Allows deleting the Auto Scaling Group without waiting for all instances in the warm pool to terminate
root.name: force_delete_warm_pool

root.description: Enables/disables detailed monitoring
root.name: enable_monitoring

root.description: Map of autoscaling group schedule to create
root.name: schedules
root.type:
map(object({
    desired_capacity = optional(number)
    end_time         = optional(string)
    max_size         = optional(number)
    min_size         = optional(number)
    recurrence       = optional(string)
    start_time       = optional(string)
    time_zone        = optional(string)
  }))

root.description: Description of the role
root.name: iam_role_description

root.description: The number of Amazon EC2 instances that should be running in the autoscaling group
root.name: desired_capacity

root.description: Determines whether to use a mixed instances policy in the autoscaling group or not
root.name: use_mixed_instances_policy

root.description: Enable Nitro Enclaves on launched instances
root.name: enclave_options

root.description: The tags to apply to the resources during launch
root.name: tag_specifications

root.description: Region where the resource(s) will be managed. Defaults to the Region set in the provider configuration
root.name: region

root.description: Reserved
root.name: context

root.description: Amount of time, in seconds, until a newly launched instance can contribute to the Amazon CloudWatch metrics. This delay lets an instance finish initializing before Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling aggregates instance metrics, resulting in more reliable usage data. Set this value equal to the amount of time that it takes for resource consumption to become stable after an instance reaches the InService state.
root.name: default_instance_warmup

root.description: Name used across the resources created
root.name: name

root.description: A list of license specifications to associate with
root.name: license_specifications

root.description: Name of launch template to be created
root.name: launch_template_name

root.description: Configuration block containing settings to define launch targets for Auto Scaling groups
root.name: mixed_instances_policy

root.description: The options for the instance hostname. The default values are inherited from the subnet
root.name: private_dns_name_options

root.description: Map of traffic source attachment definitions to create
root.name: traffic_source_attachments

root.description: A list of subnet IDs to launch resources in. Subnets automatically determine which availability zones the group will reside. Conflicts with `availability_zones`
root.name: vpc_zone_identifier

root.description: Targeting for EC2 capacity reservations
root.name: capacity_reservation_specification

root.description: If this block is configured, add a instance maintenance policy to the specified Auto Scaling group
root.name: instance_maintenance_policy

root.description: The AMI from which to launch the instance
root.name: image_id

root.description: Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of an existing IAM instance profile. Used when `create_iam_instance_profile` = `false`
root.name: iam_instance_profile_arn

root.description: IAM policies to attach to the IAM role
root.name: iam_role_policies

root.description: The name of the launch template
root.name: launch_template_name

root.description: The default version of the launch template
root.name: launch_template_default_version

root.description: List of metrics enabled for collection
root.name: autoscaling_group_enabled_metrics

root.description: Stable and unique string identifying the IAM role
root.name: iam_role_unique_id

root.description: The ID of the launch template
root.name: launch_template_id

root.description: The latest version of the launch template
root.name: launch_template_latest_version

root.description: The ARN for this AutoScaling Group
root.name: autoscaling_group_arn

root.description: The minimum size of the autoscale group
root.name: autoscaling_group_min_size

root.description: EC2 or ELB. Controls how health checking is done
root.name: autoscaling_group_health_check_type

root.description: The load balancer names associated with the autoscaling group
root.name: autoscaling_group_load_balancers

root.description: List of Target Group ARNs that apply to this AutoScaling Group
root.name: autoscaling_group_target_group_arns

root.description: Instance profile's ID
root.name: iam_instance_profile_id

root.description: The ARN of the launch template
root.name: launch_template_arn

root.description: The number of Amazon EC2 instances that should be running in the group
root.name: autoscaling_group_desired_capacity

root.description: Time after instance comes into service before checking health
root.name: autoscaling_group_health_check_grace_period

root.description: The availability zones of the autoscale group
root.name: autoscaling_group_availability_zones

root.description: The VPC zone identifier
root.name: autoscaling_group_vpc_zone_identifier

root.description: ARNs of autoscaling policies
root.name: autoscaling_policy_arns

root.description: The name of the IAM role
root.name: iam_role_name

root.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) specifying the IAM role
root.name: iam_role_arn

root.description: The autoscaling group id
root.name: autoscaling_group_id

root.description: The autoscaling group name
root.name: autoscaling_group_name

root.description: The maximum size of the autoscale group
root.name: autoscaling_group_max_size

root.description: Time between a scaling activity and the succeeding scaling activity
root.name: autoscaling_group_default_cooldown

root.description: ARNs of autoscaling group schedules
root.name: autoscaling_schedule_arns

root.description: ARN assigned by AWS to the instance profile
root.name: iam_instance_profile_arn

root.description: Stable and unique string identifying the IAM instance profile
root.name: iam_instance_profile_unique
